description: The Fatehin Unit ("Victorious") is a special forces formation under the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps), originally created in 1999 by Basij forces in West Tehran. It was formally registered in 2012 and named by then-IRGC commander Mohammad Ali Jafari. The unit specializes in special operations including sniper, commando, and counter-insurgency warfare. It has been deployed in the Syrian civil war in support of Bashar al-Assad and has been used domestically as an anti-protest force. Its commander [Mohammad Ali Fathalizadeh](/people/mohammad-ali-fathalizadeh) was killed on 1 April 2026.

sources.title: Iran: What is the IRGC Fatehin's special unit?
Basij Special Unit Fatehin and Its Role in Quelling Iran's Protests

editorial_notes: Originally a Basij special forces unit, later elevated. Deployed in Syria to protect Shrine of Lady Zaynab. Used in suppression of 2022 Mahsa Amini protests. Commander Fathalizadeh killed April 2026.
